# Video Production Plan: "What If... Doomsday"

**Generated by:** GitHub Copilot (Claude Sonnet 4.6) | **Date:** April 14, 2026

---

## Concept & Direction

**Logline:** In a fractured 2012 timeline, Tony Stark takes the sacrifice play — but the wormhole doesn't kill him. It resurrects him as something far worse. Fused with cosmic energy and his own unchecked ego, Stark returns to Earth not as a savior, but as an iron-fisted ruler: the man who saved the world and decided he alone should run it.

**Thematic Callback:** Steve Rogers' line — _"You're not the guy to make the sacrifice play"_ — recontextualized as prophecy. Rogers was right — not because Stark would fail, but because surviving it would break him.

**Aesthetic:** Cinematic Noir — chiaroscuro high-contrast lighting, oppressive rain-soaked environments, deep black shadows with only cold neon or ember-glow light sources, heavily textured metallic surfaces, desaturated color palette with selective red and green accent tones.

**Duration:** 30 seconds  
**Aspect Ratio:** 16:9 Widescreen (Cinematic Anamorphic)  
**Color Grade Reference:** Se7en (1995) × Blade Runner 2049 × Sin City  
**Audio Design:** Distorted archival Avengers dialogue → heavy rain Foley → oppressive synth-brass score → silence → Doom's voice → thunder cut-to-black

---

## Scene Breakdown & AI Prompts

---

### Scene 1: The Echo (0:00 – 0:05)

**Narrative Beat:** A crumbling Stark Tower in an alternate present. The famous 'A' on the building flickers and dies. This is not the world that was saved — this is the world that was inherited.

**Visual Direction:**

- Low-angle tilt-up shot of a rain-drenched Stark Tower, partially derelict
- Neon 'A' sign sparking erratically before cutting to total darkness
- Foreground: cracked pavement reflecting smeared neon light
- Deep blue-black color temperature, single hard-key light source from above

**Audio:**  
Steve Rogers (distorted, echoing): _"You're not the guy to make the sacrifice play…"_  
Heavy rain ambience, distant rumble of thunder.

**Nano Banana 2 — Image Prompt:**

```
Cinematic noir wide shot, Stark Tower in partial ruin on a rain-soaked New York night, low-angle upward perspective, chiaroscuro lighting with a single hard overhead light source, deep black shadows engulfing the lower half of the building, a flickering neon letter 'A' casting cold blue-white light on cracked wet pavement, reflections of dying neon in standing puddles, desaturated color palette, photorealistic gritty texture, anamorphic lens distortion, 8k --ar 16:9 --style cinematic
```

**Veo — Video Prompt:**

```
Slow cinematic tilt-up shot beginning at cracked rain-soaked pavement, rising to reveal a ruined skyscraper in a dark stormy city. Noir aesthetic, chiaroscuro lighting, deep black shadows. A flickering neon 'A' sign near the top sparks twice and extinguishes completely. Realistic rain physics, wet surface reflections, oppressive moody atmosphere. Camera movement: smooth, slow mechanical tilt. No color grading filter — achieve noir through lighting contrast only.
```

---

### Scene 2: The Sacrifice Corrupted (0:05 – 0:11)

**Narrative Beat:** Flashback — interrupted and darkened. Tony flies the nuke into the portal, but the wormhole tears open something else entirely. Instead of the void of space, a torrent of dark cosmic energy pours back through — and it wants him.

**Visual Direction:**

- Stylized flashback — desaturated except for the dark green/black energy
- Iron Man silhouette against the portal, missile gripped in both hands
- Cosmic tendrils violently reaching out and latching onto the armor
- Frame flickers and distorts, as if the memory itself is corrupted

**Audio:**  
Tony (distorted, glitching): _"We have a…"_ [cuts off] → massive low-frequency bass drop → silence.

**Nano Banana 2 — Image Prompt:**

```
Cinematic noir masterpiece, Iron Man silhouette carrying a ballistic missile flying directly into a corrupted dark wormhole, perspective from behind, the portal emitting thick coiling dark green and obsidian black supernatural energy, energy tendrils violently wrapping around and fusing with the metallic red-gold armor, distressed metal surface detail, dramatic chiaroscuro contrast, deep shadow composition, high tension atmosphere, photorealistic, 8k --ar 16:9 --style cinematic
```

**Veo — Video Prompt:**

```
Slow-motion cinematic tracking shot following an armored figure from behind as they approach a dark pulsating cosmic vortex. Deep space elements visible inside the portal but corrupted — dark green and black energy explosively erupts outward and wraps around the armor like living chains. Frame flickers and artifacts appear as if tape is damaged. Noir color grading, high contrast, oppressive score sync point at bass drop. Photorealistic metal and energy rendering.
```

---

### Scene 3: The Return (0:11 – 0:18)

**Narrative Beat:** Impact. New York pavement. Rain hammers the rubble. Beneath broken concrete and twisted rebar, something stirs — but the hand that breaks the surface is no longer recognizable as Tony Stark's.

**Visual Direction:**

- Bird's-eye crane shot descending into a smoking impact crater in a Manhattan street
- Rain cascading in sheets across shattered concrete and steam vents
- A jagged, corrupted gauntlet — gunmetal grey with green-veined cracks — punches through rubble
- Tattered dark green fabric clings to the arm
- Shallow depth of field: foreground rubble sharp, city blurred behind

**Audio:**  
Metal scraping concrete. A single deep mechanical exhale. Distant car alarms fading in the rain.

**Nano Banana 2 — Image Prompt:**

```
Cinematic noir, overhead bird's eye perspective into a rain-flooded impact crater in a New York City street at night, shattered concrete slabs and twisted rebar, steam rising from cracks, a single jagged gunmetal grey metallic gauntlet punching upward through the debris, dark green energy veins glowing faintly along seams in the armor, tattered dark green cloth draped over the arm, neon street light reflections in rain puddles, deep chiaroscuro contrast, photorealistic, 8k --ar 16:9
```

**Veo — Video Prompt:**

```
Cinematic bird's-eye crane shot slowly descending into a smoking impact crater in a rain-soaked city street at night. Rubble, steam, and heavy rain fill the frame. Suddenly a jagged robotic gauntlet violently bursts upward through concrete. Dark green energy faintly pulses through the armor's surface. Camera holds on the hand, rain shattering against distressed metal. Noir lighting, cold color temperature, high contrast. Sound design: scraping metal, mechanical breathing, distant thunder.
```

---

### Scene 4: The Iron Throne (0:18 – 0:25)

**Narrative Beat:** Wide reveal. Stark — now unrecognizable in warped armor — sits enthroned in what was once Avengers Tower. Around him: shattered Ultron sentries, Steve Rogers' shield split in two on the floor, a burning New York barely visible through floor-to-ceiling cracked glass. He rules alone.

**Visual Direction:**

- Wide cinematic shot, low angle, shooting slightly upward at the throne
- Stark centered, bathed in a single harsh top-down beam of cold white light
- Everything else falls into deep shadow
- Background: blurred dystopian cityscape, amber fires and cold blue emergency lights
- The broken shield on the floor catches a fragment of light — intentional visual irony

**Audio:**  
Slow, deliberate metallic footsteps echo in a vast empty chamber. A single imperial synth-brass note swells and holds.

**Nano Banana 2 — Image Prompt:**

```
Cinematic noir wide shot, low angle looking up at a dark armored dictator seated on an imposing throne constructed from destroyed silver-white android parts and mechanical debris, Captain America's shattered vibranium shield discarded on the wet floor in the foreground, single harsh overhead white spotlight illuminating the figure against total blackness, floor-to-ceiling shattered windows behind with blurred dystopian burning city visible, rain streaking the glass, deep chiaroscuro, photorealistic, 8k --ar 16:9
```

**Veo — Video Prompt:**

```
Slow cinematic dolly-in shot, low angle, approaching an imposing armored figure seated on a throne of destroyed robot parts in a vast dark chamber. Cracked floor-to-ceiling windows reveal a burning city skyline in the background, blurred and rain-streaked. A single cold overhead spotlight isolates the figure in deep shadow. Broken shield visible on the foreground floor. Imperial synth score swell. Noir aesthetic, heavy shadow, authoritative and terrifying presence. No camera shake — precise controlled movement.
```

---

### Scene 5: Doom Unveiled (0:25 – 0:30)

**Narrative Beat:** The camera pushes in to an extreme close-up of a new faceplate — familiar Iron Man geometry warped into something merciless. The eyes are hollow until they are not. Red light ignites. Title card drops. Thunder. Black.

**Visual Direction:**

- Extreme macro close-up, filling the entire frame with the faceplate
- Distressed gunmetal surface — scarred, rain-streaked, bearing the marks of the wormhole
- Total darkness except for the ambient glow of rain
- Eyes remain dark for 2 beats — then snap to burning, piercing red
- TITLE CARD: **WHAT IF… DOOMSDAY** (white serif, minimal, centered)
- Immediate cut to black on thunder crack

**Audio:**  
Stark's new synthesized, sub-bass voice: _"I made the play. Now, I make the rules."_  
Thunder crack → hard cut to silence.

**Nano Banana 2 — Image Prompt:**

```
Cinematic noir extreme close up macro photograph of a terrifying metallic battle mask, Iron Man helmet geometry corrupted and warped toward Doctor Doom archetype, heavily distressed gunmetal grey surface with dark green energy cracks, vertical scarring from cosmic forces, rain droplets hitting the metal surface mid-fall frozen in time, eye slits filled with burning sinister red light cutting through total darkness, deep chiaroscuro, no ambient fill light, photorealistic metal textures, ultra detailed surface damage, 8k --ar 16:9 --style cinematic
```

**Veo — Video Prompt:**

```
Extreme macro close-up push-in shot on a scarred gunmetal metallic mask filling the entire frame. Total darkness except for ambient rain catching faint light on the metal surface. Rain drops strike the mask in slow motion. Camera incrementally drifts closer. Two beats of silence — then both eye slits suddenly ignite with harsh burning red light. Camera holds. Cut to black immediately on a thunder crack. Cinematic noir, zero ambient fill light, chiaroscuro, photorealistic metal rendering, terrifying reveal pacing.
```

## Production Notes

- **Nano Banana 2** generations should use seed locking across scenes to maintain facial/armor consistency in Scenes 3–5.
- **Veo** clips should be exported at minimum 24fps; 48fps for slow-motion scenes (2, 3) then retimed in post.
- All scenes benefit from a **slight warping/lens breathing** effect in post to reinforce the corrupted timeline aesthetic.
- Scene 2 benefits from **VHS-style frame dropout** overlaid at low opacity (10–15%) to communicate the corrupted memory concept.
- Final color grade target: crush blacks to pure black, desaturate mids, retain red and green as the only saturated accent colors throughout.

---

---

# STEP-BY-STEP PRODUCTION GUIDE

### _As directed by a Marvel Studios Video & Sound Editor_

> **Mindset:** You are not making a fan edit. You are making a proof-of-concept short that lives inside the Marvel What If...? universe. Every decision — frame, sound, cut — must feel intentional. Treat every second like it costs $80,000 to render.

---

## PHASE 1 — PRE-PRODUCTION (Before you touch any software)

---

### STEP 1 — Gather Your Source Audio

You need two pieces of distorted archival audio before anything else. These are your emotional anchors.

**Audio Asset 1 — Steve Rogers Line**

- Source: _The Avengers (2012)_ — "You're not the guy to make the sacrifice play."
- How to isolate: Use **VoiceAI** (voiceai.com) or **Adobe Podcast Enhance** to strip background score if needed
- Effect to apply later: Pitch shift -2 semitones + reverb (large hall, 3.5s decay) + low-pass filter at 4kHz → sounds like a memory being played back in a broken mind

**Audio Asset 2 — Tony's Corrupted Line (Synthesized)**

- _"I made the play. Now, I make the rules."_
- You will generate this using **ElevenLabs** → clone from Robert Downey Jr. interviews (publicly available vocal reference)
- Voice settings: Stability 85%, Similarity 90%, Style Exaggeration 40%
- Post-process: Add pitch shift -3 semitones + vocoder/ring modulator (Izotope Nectar or free alternative: **TAL-Vocoder**) → this creates the "Doom voice" effect, mechanical and inhuman
- Layer over: a 60Hz sub-bass drone at -18dBFS continuously under the line

**Audio Asset 3 — Score / Music Bed**

- Use **Suno AI** or **Udio** to generate a custom 30-second noir orchestral cue
- Prompt for Suno: `"Hans Zimmer dark noir, no melody, slow building synth brass, oppressive low strings, single imperial horn note at 0:18, thunder percussion at 0:29, 30 seconds, no vocals, cinematic"`
- Export at 48kHz / 24-bit WAV

**Audio Asset 4 — Foley / Ambience**

- Rain loop (heavy, stereo): [freesound.org](https://freesound.org) — search "heavy rain city night"
- Metal scrape: search "metal debris scrape concrete"
- Mechanical breathing: search "respirator industrial breathing"
- Thunder crack (single, sharp): search "close thunder crack mono"
- Car alarms fading: search "distant car alarm rain"
- All assets: download as WAV, minimum 44.1kHz

---

### STEP 2 — Generate All AI Visuals

Work scene-by-scene in this exact order. Do not skip ahead.

**For Nano Banana 2 (Image Generation):**

1. Open [Nano Banana 2](https://www.nanobananai.com) or your access point
2. Generate Scene 5 (the faceplate) **first** — this is your "hero asset." Get it perfect before anything else
3. Once happy with Scene 5, use its seed number to maintain armor visual consistency for Scenes 3 and 4
4. Generate 4–6 variations per scene, select the highest contrast / deepest shadow result
5. Save all outputs as PNG at maximum resolution
6. Name files: `scene01_echo.png`, `scene02_sacrifice.png`, etc.

**For Veo (Video Generation):**

1. Access via **Google VideoFX** / **Google AI Test Kitchen** or API
2. Generate each scene's video prompt — set duration to the target seconds per scene
3. For Scene 2 (slow motion): generate at standard speed, you will retime in editing
4. For Scene 5: generate just the eye-ignite moment as a separate 3-second clip
5. Export all clips as **MP4 H.264** minimum — ProRes if available
6. Name files: `scene01_v01.mp4`, `scene02_v01.mp4`, etc.

**Pro Tip:** If a Veo clip doesn't match the image perfectly, use the Nano Banana 2 image as an **image-to-video reference frame** input in Veo for visual continuity.

---

## PHASE 2 — EDITING (Assembly)

**Recommended Software:** DaVinci Resolve 19 (free version is sufficient — this is what Marvel's finishing editors use for color)  
**Alternative:** CapCut Pro (mobile-first, faster for quick assembly)  
**Project Settings:** 1920x1080, 23.976fps, 48kHz audio, Rec.709 timeline

---

### STEP 3 — Build the Timeline (Rough Cut)

1. Create a new project: `WhatIf_Doomsday_v001`
2. Import all video clips and PNG images into the Media Pool
3. Create a new timeline: 30 seconds, 23.976fps
4. Drop all clips in sequence using the Scene Breakdown timecodes:

| Track | Content                   | In   | Out  |
| ----- | ------------------------- | ---- | ---- |
| V1    | scene01_v01.mp4           | 0:00 | 0:05 |
| V1    | scene02_v01.mp4           | 0:05 | 0:11 |
| V1    | scene03_v01.mp4           | 0:11 | 0:18 |
| V1    | scene04_v01.mp4           | 0:18 | 0:25 |
| V1    | scene05_v01.mp4           | 0:25 | 0:29 |
| V1    | Title card (black + text) | 0:29 | 0:30 |

5. Watch it through once with no audio. Check pacing — each cut should feel like it was **forced on you**, not invited.

---

### STEP 4 — Add Transitions (Zero Tolerance for Lazy Cuts)

> Marvel editors do not use dissolves for action. Every cut is a **statement**.

- Scene 1 → Scene 2: **Hard cut** — on the exact frame the 'A' goes dark
- Scene 2 → Scene 3: **Flash frame** (1 single white frame) on the bass drop, then hard cut into darkness
- Scene 3 → Scene 4: **Hard cut** — on the mechanical exhale
- Scene 4 → Scene 5: **Slow push whip** — in post, use a 4-frame zoom punch (scale 100% → 103% over 4 frames) to simulate a crash zoom into the faceplate
- Scene 5 → Title: **Instant black** on thunder frame — zero fade, zero dissolve

---

### STEP 5 — Scene 2 VHS Corruption Effect

This scene represents a corrupted memory. Apply this effect stack in Resolve:

1. Add a **Noise** node (OpenFX → Resolve FX → Noise) — Grain size 2.0, strength 0.08
2. Add a **Lens Distortion** node — barrel distortion 0.03, chromatic aberration 0.5
3. On V2 track above, place a **VHS overlay texture** (search freebies on Motion Array or Envato) at **12% opacity, Overlay blend mode**
4. Add a **Horizontal Rolling Shutter** keyframe glitch: at 0:07 and 0:09, animate horizontal position ±4px over 2 frames each → simulates tape dropout

---

## PHASE 3 — SOUND DESIGN (The Most Important Phase)

> "In a Marvel film, the sound IS the emotion. The picture just confirms it." — Every Marvel sound supervisor ever.

---

### STEP 6 — Build the Audio Timeline

In DaVinci Resolve Fairlight (or import into **Adobe Audition** / **Audacity**):

**Track Layout:**

| Track | Content                        | Pan                  |
| ----- | ------------------------------ | -------------------- |
| A1    | Music bed (Suno score)         | Center               |
| A2    | Rain ambience loop             | Wide stereo (L+R)    |
| A3    | Rogers VO (processed)          | Center, slight left  |
| A4    | Tony/Doom VO (processed)       | Center, slight right |
| A5    | Foley SFX (metal, breath)      | Center               |
| A6    | Thunder crash                  | Center (mono, hard)  |
| A7    | Sub-bass drone (under Doom VO) | Center               |

**Level Targets (LUFS for social/web export):**

- Music bed: -18 LUFS (give dialogue room to breathe)
- VO (Rogers): -12 LUFS peak
- VO (Doom): -10 LUFS peak (this voice should feel **louder than the world**)
- Thunder crack: -6 LUFS (peaked, intentionally shocking)
- Master output target: -14 LUFS integrated (YouTube/Instagram standard)

---

### STEP 7 — Process Each Audio Layer

**Rogers VO processing chain (in order):**

1. EQ: cut everything above 5kHz (dull it, like a fading memory)
2. Reverb: large hall, pre-delay 30ms, decay 3.5s, wet 40%
3. Pitch: -2 semitones
4. Bitcrusher (Audacity: "Nyquist Prompt" distortion): subtle, 5% — adds analog decay
5. Fade in over 8 frames from silence

**Doom VO processing chain (in order):**

1. Pitch: -3 semitones
2. Vocoder / Ring modulator: TAL-Vocoder free VST, carrier frequency 80Hz
3. EQ: boost 80–200Hz (+4dB), cut 3–8kHz (-3dB), slight boost 10kHz (+2dB) — makes it large and metallic
4. Limiter: ceiling -1.0dBFS
5. Layer: 60Hz sine wave sub-drone at -22dBFS running under the full line duration

**Rain ambience:**

- Stereo field: spread wide (L 100%, R 100%)
- EQ: cut below 80Hz (rumble removal), slight boost 2–4kHz (make the rain articulate and sharp — you want each drop to feel cold)
- Fade in at 0:00, constant through 0:29, fade out instantly on thunder

**Thunder crack:**

- Mono, center only
- Add 150ms of silence before placing it — the half-second of silence before thunder is what makes it terrifying
- No reverb needed — this one should feel the most real thing in the whole piece

---

### STEP 8 — Score Integration & Music Sync Points

Lock your music sync hits to these exact timecodes:

| Timecode | Music Event                  | Why                                     |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| 0:00     | Score starts at near-silence | Let Rogers VO breathe                   |
| 0:08     | Bass drop hit                | Synchronized to Scene 2 flash frame cut |
| 0:11     | Score pulls back to minimal  | Allow Foley to dominate                 |
| 0:18     | Imperial brass swell begins  | Scene 4 throne reveal                   |
| 0:24     | Score cuts completely        | Silence before the reveal               |
| 0:28     | Sub-bass drone fades in      | Under Doom VO                           |
| 0:29     | Thunder crack                | Hard sync — everything stops here       |

---

## PHASE 4 — COLOR GRADING

---

### STEP 9 — Apply the Noir Grade in DaVinci Resolve

Go to the **Color** page. For every clip, apply this node tree in order:

**Node 1 — Normalize:**

- Lift shadows slightly to see clip detail before grading
- Set white balance to a cool/blue cast (approximately 5500K → 4200K)

**Node 2 — Crushed Blacks:**

- Lift (shadows): drag down until blacks are **truly black** — no grey shadow detail
- Gamma (mids): drag down 0.5 stops
- Gain (highlights): drag up to 1.1 — you want one harsh bright area, everything else dark

**Node 3 — Desaturation:**

- Hue vs. Saturation: pull ALL hues to near-zero **except:**
  - Red: keep at 80% saturation (Doom's eyes, fire)
  - Green/Teal: keep at 60% saturation (cosmic energy)
- This is your entire visual color language — red and green only

**Node 4 — Film Grain:**

- Resolve FX Noise → Luma noise only, grain size 1.8, strength 0.05
- This gives it the Se7en 1995 photochemical film texture

**Node 5 — Vignette:**

- Hard oval vignette, midpoint 0.55, softness 0.4, strength -0.6
- Every frame should feel like you're looking through a tunnel

---

## PHASE 5 — TITLE CARD & EXPORT

---

### STEP 10 — Create the Title Card

In Resolve (or After Effects / CapCut):

1. Create a solid black background clip, duration: 18 frames (0:29:00 – 0:30:00)
2. Add text: `WHAT IF…` (smaller, lighter weight, tracking +200)
3. Add text below: `DOOMSDAY` (larger, heavier weight, tracking +400)
4. Font: **Trajan Pro** (the Marvel standard) or Big Caslon / Cinzel
5. Color: Pure white `#FFFFFF`
6. Animation: opacity 0% → 100% over 6 frames, hold 10 frames, hard cut (no fade out — the video just ends)
7. **Optional:** Add a very faint red glow (outer glow effect, red `#8B0000`, spread 40px, opacity 15%) on "DOOMSDAY" only

---

### STEP 11 — Export Settings

**For YouTube / Instagram Reels:**

| Setting           | Value                 |
| ----------------- | --------------------- |
| Container         | MP4                   |
| Video Codec       | H.264                 |
| Resolution        | 1920 × 1080           |
| Frame Rate        | 23.976fps             |
| Bitrate           | 20 Mbps (VBR, 2-pass) |
| Audio Codec       | AAC                   |
| Audio Sample Rate | 48kHz                 |
| Audio Bitrate     | 320 kbps              |
| Color Space       | Rec.709               |
| Master LUFS       | -14 integrated        |

**For TikTok:**

- Same settings but add a letterbox matte (top/bottom black bars) to force the cinematic frame even on vertical platforms

---

## PHASE 6 — FINAL QC CHECKLIST

Before you render, run through this list exactly as a Marvel finishing editor would:

- [ ] Every black in the frame is **true black** (RGB 0,0,0) — no crushed-but-not-black greys
- [ ] Red and green are the **only** saturated colors visible in any frame
- [ ] Rogers VO is intelligible despite processing (play it on phone speakers to test)
- [ ] Doom VO lands **louder in perceived weight** than Rogers VO
- [ ] The bass drop at 0:08 is felt, not just heard — test on a subwoofer or headphones
- [ ] The 150ms silence before thunder at 0:29 is preserved — do not cut it
- [ ] Title card is on screen for exactly 18 frames — not 17, not 19
- [ ] Total duration is **exactly 30:00 seconds** at 23.976fps
- [ ] Grain is visible but not distracting — if you notice it consciously, reduce it
- [ ] Export file size is under 500MB (for social uploads)

---

> **Final Note from the Editor's Chair:**  
> The 2-second silence between Doom's last word and the thunder is the whole film.  
> That silence is Tony Stark realizing what he's become — and not caring.  
> Protect that silence in every version you export. Everything else is craft. That moment is art.
